titleStmt.title: Letter from Aradŋu to Šulgi about the fortress Igi-ḫursaŋa -- an English prose translation

publicationStmt.distributor: The Electronic Text Corpus of Sumerian Literature

address.addrLine: The Oriental Institute, University of Oxford, Pusey Lane, Oxford OX1 2LE, UK, email etcsl@orinst.ox.ac.uk, http://etcsl.orinst.ox.ac.uk

seriesStmt.p: The Electronic Text Corpus of Sumerian Literature aims to make accessible over 400 literary works composed in the Sumerian language in ancient Mesopotamia during the late third and early second millennia BC. The works are in Sumerian composite transliteration and English prose translation with bibliographical information on each composition.

sourceDesc.p: Sources are listed in the header of the composite text

div1.p: Say to my lord: This is what Aradŋu, your servant, says:
My lord, your word is the word of An, it ……. Your decreed destiny has been bestowed on you as on a god.

As to the fortification which my lord sent me back to, the work on it has been put into effect. The approach of the enemy is kept at a distance from the Land. My lord continues to maintain his sublime reputation in the south and the uplands, from the rising to the setting sun, as far as the borders of the entire Land. The rebellious (?) Martu have turned back ……
an Akkadian gloss has instead: The totality …… .

Kurgamabi
an Akkadian gloss has instead: Kunšī-mātum …… to Šulgi. The fortress Igi-ḫursaŋa ……. And who will rival him ……?

……
an Akkadian gloss has instead: The letter of my lord ……. Whatever ……, my lord, ……. My lord should ……!
